lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Hi, can I get a small iced americano?
lines.ko: 안녕하세요, 스몰 아이스 아메리카노 하나 주시겠어요?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Sure. For here or to go?

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: To go, please.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: That'll be four fifty.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Here you go. Keep the change.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Thanks! Have a good one.

note: for here or to go는 미국 카페 어디서나 듣는 필수 질문. Keep the change는 팁 문화권 표현

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Hey, are you free this Saturday?
lines.ko: 야, 이번 주 토요일에 시간 돼?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: I think so. What's up?

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Want to grab dinner? How about 7?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Sounds good. Where should we go?

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: That new pasta place downtown?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Perfect. See you there!

note: 약속 잡을 때 가장 흔한 대화 흐름: 시간 확인 → 제안 → 장소 정하기

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Excuse me, how do I get to the subway station?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Just go straight and turn left at the light.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Is it far from here?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Not really, about five minutes on foot.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Great, thank you so much!

note: 길을 묻고 답할 때 가장 흔히 쓰는 패턴

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Hi, can I get a medium latte, please?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Sure. For here or to go?

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: To go, please. Oh, and can you make it iced?
lines.ko: 포장이요. 아, 아이스로 해주실 수 있나요?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: No problem. That'll be four fifty.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Here you go. Keep the change.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Thanks! It'll be ready in a few minutes.
lines.ko: 감사합니다! 몇 분 안에 준비될 거예요.

note: 카페에서는 for here or to go를 거의 항상 물어보니까 미리 답 준비해두면 편함

key_expressions: I'd like to make a reservation

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Hi, I'd like to make a reservation for tonight.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Sure, for how many people?

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Four, around seven o'clock.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Let me check... we're full at seven. Do you have anything later?
lines.ko: 잠시만요... 7시는 자리가 다 찼네요. 조금 늦게 오실 수 있으세요?

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Sure, how about seven thirty?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: That works. Can I get your name?

note: 예약할 때 인원수랑 시간부터 물어보니까 미리 정해두고 전화하면 좋음

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Hi there! I just moved in next door.
lines.ko: 안녕하세요! 저 옆집에 새로 이사 왔어요.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Oh, welcome! I'm Rachel, I live right across the hall.
lines.ko: 아, 환영해요! 저는 레이첼이에요, 바로 맞은편에 살아요.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Nice to meet you. I'm Kevin.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: If you ever need anything, just knock on my door.
lines.ko: 뭐 필요한 거 있으면 언제든 문 두드리세요.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: That's so kind, thank you. I'll be sure to do that.

note: 새 이웃한테는 짧게 이름 소개하고 도움 필요하면 말하라는 식으로 마무리하는 게 자연스러움

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Hey, are you free for dinner this week?
lines.ko: 야, 이번 주에 저녁 시간 돼?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Yeah, I think so. How about Thursday?
lines.ko: 응, 될 것 같아. 목요일 어때?

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Thursday works for me. Where do you want to go?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: There's a new Thai place downtown. Let's meet at seven.
lines.ko: 시내에 새로 생긴 태국 음식점 있잖아. 7시에 만나자.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Sounds perfect. See you then!

note: 약속 잡을 때 요일이랑 시간 딱 정해서 말하면 서로 편함

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Excuse me, do you have this in a medium?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Let me check the back. Yes, we have it.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Great, can I try it on?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Of course, the fitting room is right over there.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Actually, it's a bit tight. Do you have a large instead?
lines.ko: 음, 이거 좀 끼는데요. 라지 사이즈로 있을까요?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Sure, I'll grab one for you.

note: 사이즈 안 맞을 때는 It's a bit tight/loose라고 편하게 말하면 됨

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Hi, can you take me to the airport?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Sure thing. Which terminal?

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Terminal two, please. How long will it take?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: With traffic, maybe thirty minutes.
lines.ko: 차 막히는 거 감안하면 한 30분 정도요.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: That's fine. Right here is fine, thanks.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: No problem, have a safe flight.

note: 목적지 말할 때는 세부 장소(터미널, 출입구 등)까지 같이 말해주면 훨씬 편함

key_expressions: Could I get a room with

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Hi, I have a reservation under the name Sophie Lee.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Let me pull that up... yes, two nights, one queen bed.
lines.ko: 확인해볼게요... 네, 2박에 퀸사이즈 침대 하나로 되어 있네요.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: That's right. Could I get a room with a view, if possible?
lines.ko: 맞아요. 혹시 가능하면 전망 좋은 방으로 받을 수 있을까요?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: I can put you on a higher floor, no extra charge.
lines.ko: 높은 층으로 배정해드릴게요, 추가 비용은 없어요.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Perfect. What time is checkout?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Checkout is at eleven. Here's your key card.

note: 체크인할 때 예약 이름 먼저 말하고 원하는 요청은 자연스럽게 덧붙이면 됨

key_expressions: I'd like to see a doctor
Is this your first time here

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Hi, I'd like to see a doctor. I've had a sore throat for days.
lines.ko: 안녕하세요, 진료 좀 받고 싶은데요. 목이 며칠째 아파서요.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Is this your first time here?

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Yes, it is.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Okay, please fill out this form and have a seat.
lines.ko: 네, 이 서류 작성하시고 잠시 앉아 계세요.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Sure. About how long is the wait?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: About fifteen minutes. We'll call your name.
lines.ko: 한 15분 정도요. 성함 부르면 들어가시면 돼요.

note: 접수할 때 증상 간단히 말하고 첫 방문인지 물으면 대답만 잘하면 됨

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Hi, just these two, please.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Is that everything for you today?

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Yes, that's it.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Do you want a bag for that?

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: No thanks, I'll just carry it.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Okay, that'll be three dollars. Here's your change.

note: 간단한 계산 상황이라 짧고 반복되는 표현 위주라 익혀두면 편함

key_expressions: I love this kind of weather

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Nice weather today, isn't it?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Yeah, it's perfect. I heard it's supposed to rain tomorrow though.
lines.ko: 응, 완전 좋아. 근데 내일은 비 온다더라.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Really? That's a shame, I was going to go hiking.
lines.ko: 진짜? 아쉽다, 나 등산 가려고 했는데.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Maybe do it today instead. I love this kind of weather.
lines.ko: 그럼 오늘 가는 게 어때. 나 이런 날씨 완전 좋아해.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: That's a good idea, actually.

note: 영어권에서는 날씨 얘기가 진짜 흔한 스몰토크 소재라 짧게 던지기 좋음

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Hi, I'm calling to change my appointment.
lines.ko: 안녕하세요, 예약 시간 좀 바꾸려고 전화드렸어요.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Sure, can I get your name and current appointment time?
lines.ko: 네, 성함이랑 원래 예약 시간 알려주시겠어요?

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: It's Daniel Park, I'm booked for two on Friday.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Got it. What time would you like instead?

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Would it be possible to move it to four?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Yes, that's open. All set for four on Friday.
lines.ko: 네, 그 시간 비어 있어요. 금요일 4시로 변경됐습니다.

note: 전화로 일정 바꿀 땐 원래 시간이랑 원하는 시간을 딱 말해주면 빨리 처리됨

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Hi, here's my passport and reservation.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Thank you. Aisle or window seat?
lines.ko: 감사합니다. 통로 쪽 좋으세요, 창가 쪽 좋으세요?

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Window, please, if it's available.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: You're all set, seat 24A. Any bags to check?

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Just this one suitcase. Where's the gate?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Gate fourteen, boarding starts at nine thirty.

note: 체크인할 때 좌석 선호랑 짐 개수 미리 생각해두면 수속이 빨리 끝남

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Hi, I'd like to sign up for a membership.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Great, what plans do you have in mind? Monthly or annual?
lines.ko: 좋아요, 어떤 플랜 생각하세요? 월 단위요, 아니면 연 단위요?

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: I'm not sure yet. Is there a trial period?
lines.ko: 아직 잘 모르겠어요. 체험 기간 같은 게 있나요?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Yes, we offer a free three-day trial.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Perfect, let's start with that.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Sounds good. I'll just need your ID and phone number.

note: 등록 전에 체험 기간부터 물어보는 게 자연스러운 순서

key_expressions: I'd like to open an account
What do I need to bring

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Hi, I'd like to open a checking account.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Sure. What do I need to bring, you mean documents?
lines.ko: 네. 어떤 게 필요하신지, 서류 말씀이신 거죠?

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Right, what do I need to bring for that?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Just a photo ID and proof of address.
lines.ko: 신분증이랑 주소 증명 서류만 있으면 돼요.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Got it. Is there a monthly fee?
lines.ko: 알겠어요. 매달 나가는 수수료 같은 거 있나요?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: No fee if you keep a minimum balance of five hundred.

note: 계좌 관련 문의는 필요 서류랑 수수료부터 물어보면 됨

key_expressions: I'd like to send this package
How long will it take to arrive

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Hi, I'd like to send this package to Korea.
lines.ko: 안녕하세요, 이 소포 한국으로 보내고 싶은데요.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Sure, let's put it on the scale. That'll be around thirty dollars.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: How long will it take to arrive?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: About a week with standard shipping.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: That's fine. Do you need it insured?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: It's optional, but I'd recommend it for electronics.
lines.ko: 선택 사항인데, 전자기기면 드는 걸 추천드려요.

note: 소포 보낼 때는 무게 재고 가격, 도착 기간, 보험 순으로 물어보면 자연스러움

key_expressions: Can we sit near the back

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Hi, two tickets for the seven o'clock showing, please.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Sure. What time does it start, did you say seven?

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Yes, seven. Can we sit near the back?
lines.ko: 네, 7시요. 뒤쪽 자리로 앉을 수 있을까요?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Let me check the seating chart... row J works.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Perfect, we'll take that.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Great, that'll be twenty-four dollars total.

note: 영화표 살 때 시간이랑 좌석 위치 순서로 얘기하면 됨

key_expressions: I'd like to book an appointment

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Hi, I'd like to book an appointment for a haircut.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Sure, do you have a stylist in mind?

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Not really. Do you have any openings this weekend?
lines.ko: 딱히 없어요. 이번 주말에 예약 가능한 시간 있나요?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: We have Saturday at eleven or Sunday at two.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Saturday works. How much does it cost?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: A haircut is thirty-five dollars.

note: 미용실 예약은 원하는 시술이랑 가능한 시간대부터 확인하면 편함

key_expressions: Excuse me, where can I find
Do you have any in the back

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Excuse me, where can I find olive oil?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: It's in aisle six, next to the vinegar.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Thanks. Also, do you have any in the back? The shelf looks empty.
lines.ko: 감사해요. 그리고 혹시 창고에 더 있나요? 선반이 비어 보여서요.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Let me check for you real quick.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: I appreciate it.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Good news, we have more. I'll bring some out.

note: 마트에서는 aisle 번호로 위치 설명하니까 이 표현만 알아둬도 충분함

key_expressions: Hope all your wishes come true

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Happy birthday, Grace! You look amazing tonight.
lines.ko: 생일 축하해, 그레이스! 오늘 진짜 예쁘다.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Aw, thank you so much for coming!

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Of course. I got you something, it's not much.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: You didn't have to! Can I open it now?
lines.ko: 안 그래도 되는데! 지금 열어봐도 돼?

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Go ahead! Hope all your wishes come true this year.
lines.ko: 열어봐! 올해는 소원 다 이루어지길 바라.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: That's so sweet of you, thank you.

note: 생일 파티에서는 칭찬 한마디랑 선물, 덕담을 자연스럽게 이어서 말하면 됨
